changes.detail: All five mandatory fields present and valid. work_id=LC-S2-CONTEXT-INTEGRATION, branch=feature/lc-s2-context-integration, risk_level=HIGH, execution_model=TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH, change_type=MIXED. HIGH is correct: touches orchestrator.py (Intelligence Core, SOP §3), narrative_report_compiler_v1.py (Intelligence Core), AnalysisDTO contract (output generation surface). MIXED is correct: questionnaire.json addition is CONTENT; orchestrator/mapper/DTO/compiler wiring is BEHAVIOUR; SOP §4.2 requires MIXED when both are present. TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH is required for all HIGH-risk work.

Statin gate pack confirmed. Gate closed 2026-05-09 with all 6 decisions CLOSED: statin capture (§4.1), governed truth location (§4.2), modifier engine architecture (§4.3), affected outputs (§4.4), allowed effect type (§4.5), Layer B/C boundary (§4.6). Proving checks S-1 through S-6 approved (§4.8). Gate outcome: CLOSED. This is the binding prerequisite authority for Sprint 2.

lipid_lowering_statin registry entry read in full. Three interpretation_effects: (1) expected_biomarker_effect on [ldl_cholesterol, non_hdl_cholesterol, apob], direction: lower; (2) monitoring_relevance on [alt, ast, creatine_kinase], direction: variable; (3) caveat_only on [hdl_cholesterol], direction: variable. evidence_strength: strong. Registry status: LOCKED. No changes permitted in Sprint 2.

Statin alias section confirmed. 11 aliases: atorvastatin, crestor, fluvastatin, lescol, lipitor, lovastatin, pitavastatin, pravastatin, rosuvastatin, simvastatin, zocor — all mapping to lipid_lowering_statin. Status: LOCKED. No changes permitted in Sprint 2.

lipid_lowering_statin confirmed in _APPROVED_INTERVENTION_CLASS_IDS set at line 21-22. Compiler build_intervention_annotations_v1() signature at line 80-82 accepts Optional[Dict[str, Any]] user_intervention_document. Emits InterventionAnnotationsV1 with resolved/unresolved lists. No changes required to this file — it is production-grade and governed.

InterventionAnnotationsV1 contract confirmed. Fields: version (Literal['v1']), registry_id (str), registry_schema_version (str), resolved (List[InterventionAnnotationResolvedV1]), unresolved (List[InterventionAnnotationUnresolvedV1]). model_config extra='forbid'. InterventionAnnotationResolvedV1 contains: intervention_record_id, entered_label, intervention_class_id, effects (List[InterventionAnnotationEffectV1]). This is the contract shape Cursor must use for AnalysisDTO extension.

user_intervention_document LOCKED schema confirmed. Root required: schema_version ('1.0.0'), intervention_records. Record required: intervention_record_id, intervention_type (medication|supplement|lifestyle|behavioural|clinical), entered_label, canonical_class (with link_status: mapped|unmapped), timeline (effective_from_date, effective_to_date, is_ongoing, change_event_type: started|stopped|changed), provenance (source_type: user_reported|clinician_prescribed|inferred, confidence: confirmed|estimated|unknown). Fixture reference at backend/tests/fixtures/user_intervention_exposure/valid_record_set.yaml — EXISTS and confirmed as canonical shape reference.

long_term_medications question at lines 146-153. Current options read from file: ['None', 'Corticosteroids', 'Atypical antipsychotics', 'HIV/AIDS treatments']. Statin option is ABSENT. Gate pack §4.1 claim verified by direct file read. Sprint Task A must add 'Statins (cholesterol medication)' to this options array.

long_term_medications handling at lines 161-165 maps to List[str] (long_term_medication_classes) only. No path to user_intervention_document. Gate pack §4.1/§4.3 claim verified. Sprint Task B must extend the mapper to build a schema-compliant user_intervention_document when 'Statins (cholesterol medication)' is selected.

compile_narrative_report_v1() call at lines 2201-2206: parameters are analysis_id, meta, insight_graph, idl_bundle. No user_intervention_document or intervention_annotations_v1 parameter. Gate pack §4.3 claim verified. Sprint Task C must wire user_intervention_document through the orchestrator and resolve the annotation before or during compile_narrative_report_v1() invocation.

compile_narrative_report_v1() function signature at lines 522-528 accepts: analysis_id, meta, insight_graph, idl_bundle. No user_intervention_document or intervention_annotations_v1 parameter. Gate pack §4.3 claim verified. Sprint Task E/F requires this function to accept the annotation and use it to produce statin-contextualised narrative content.

AnalysisDTO fields read at lines 245-298. No intervention_annotations_v1 field present. model_config: frozen=True, extra='forbid'. Gate pack §4.3 claim verified. Sprint Task D must add intervention_annotations_v1: Optional[InterventionAnnotationsV1] = Field(default=None) to AnalysisDTO. Import from core.contracts.intervention_annotation_v1 required. The Optional + default=None pattern is backward-compatible with frozen=True and extra='forbid'.

Single registry file. No duplicate intervention authority source detected. The legacy compile_report_v1() wiring at report_compiler_v1.py:774 also calls build_intervention_annotations_v1() but this is the legacy (non-production) path — not a duplicate authority source for the governed registry.

Gate pack §4.1 describes mapper output as 'intervention_class_id: lipid_lowering_statin, link_status: mapped'. LOCKED schema requires full document shape including all timeline and provenance fields. Cursor must build a complete schema-valid document. Recommended field values for questionnaire-derived statin: schema_version='1.0.0', intervention_record_id='rec_statin_01' (or generated), intervention_type='medication', entered_label='Statins (cholesterol medication)', canonical_class.link_status='mapped', canonical_class.intervention_class_id='lipid_lowering_statin', timeline.is_ongoing=true, timeline.change_event_type='started', timeline.effective_from_date=null, timeline.effective_to_date=null, provenance.source_type='user_reported', provenance.confidence='estimated'. Prompt instruction 'real production document shape' adequately covers this — treating as WARNING not BLOCKER.

The sprint must carry the statin annotation to both AnalysisDTO and the narrative compiler. Two approaches exist: (a) orchestrator calls build_intervention_annotations_v1() once and passes the InterventionAnnotationsV1 object to both; (b) narrative compiler calls it internally from the raw document (loads registry twice). Architecture (a) is correct: build once, carry the object. This avoids redundant registry loads and is consistent with determinism principles. Cursor must not call build_intervention_annotations_v1() twice for the same analysis run.

authority_files_verified.note: Gate pack. Closed 2026-05-09. All 6 decisions CLOSED. Binding authority for Sprint 2 scope.
LOCKED. lipid_lowering_statin entry at lines 9-35 read in full. 3 effect types confirmed.
LOCKED. 11 statin aliases at lines 22-43 confirmed.
lipid_lowering_statin in _APPROVED_INTERVENTION_CLASS_IDS at line 22. build_intervention_annotations_v1() confirmed. No changes required.
InterventionAnnotationsV1 contract confirmed. extra='forbid'. Full schema read.
LOCKED. All required fields confirmed. Forbidden keys confirmed (threshold, override_signal, signal_state_mutation, etc.).
Fixture confirmed. Shows full schema-compliant document shape. Use as Cursor reference for mapper implementation.
long_term_medications at lines 146-153. Statin absent confirmed. Sprint Task A target.
long_term_medications mapped to List[str] at lines 161-165. No user_intervention_document path. Sprint Task B target.
compile_narrative_report_v1() call at lines 2201-2206. No intervention_document arg. Sprint Task C target.
compile_narrative_report_v1() signature at lines 522-528. No intervention param. Sprint Tasks E/F target.
AnalysisDTO at lines 245-298. No intervention_annotations_v1 field. Sprint Task D target.

intelligence_core_components_identified: backend/core/pipeline/orchestrator.py — wiring user_intervention_document + annotation build through live pipeline
backend/core/analytics/narrative_report_compiler_v1.py — new param + statin annotation consumption for narrative output
backend/core/models/results.py (AnalysisDTO) — new intervention_annotations_v1 field (output contract change)
backend/core/pipeline/questionnaire_mapper.py — new user_intervention_document building path (BEHAVIOUR: input-to-governed-contract translation)
stage_2b.behavioural_surface_area: compile_narrative_report_v1() gains a new parameter and produces different output for statin-on profiles in consumer-facing fields (lead_narrative, clinician section). AnalysisDTO gains intervention_annotations_v1 Optional field (default None — backward compatible). All statin-off profiles: output unchanged. Signal states, rankings, domain bands: must be identical between statin-on and statin-off (S-6).
stage_2b.expected_output_changes: Statin-on profiles: (1) AnalysisDTO.intervention_annotations_v1 populated with lipid_lowering_statin resolved annotation; (2) at least one consumer-visible field in lead_narrative or clinician_report_v1.sections.page1 differs from statin-off. Statin-off profiles: AnalysisDTO.intervention_annotations_v1=None, no narrative change. Rankings/signals/bands: unchanged in both profiles.
regression_targets: Existing golden panel tests — statin-off profile must produce identical AnalysisDTO output on all pre-existing fields

New tests required: S-1 questionnaire capture, S-2 mapper output, S-3 annotation compiler resolution, S-4 pipeline carriage, S-5 statin-on vs statin-off visible difference, S-6 no analytical truth mutation
determinism_risks: Narrative statin prose: must be derived deterministically from the intervention registry annotation only — no LLM, no hardcoded freeform strings. Mitigated by Layer B/C boundary instruction in prompt.

user_intervention_document construction: timeline fields (effective_from_date, is_ongoing) must use consistent, reproducible defaults — not system time or random values. Mitigated by Warning W-1 guidance.

AnalysisDTO frozen=True: adding Optional field with default None is backward-compatible; any Cursor deviation (e.g. required field) would break existing tests immediately. Mitigated by explicit 'Optional' instruction in Task D.
Build-once risk: if annotation is built twice (orchestrator + narrative compiler), registry may be loaded twice — not a determinism risk but an architectural smell. Mitigated by Warning W-2 guidance.
